references.reason: Aya's office is where a report begins with explicit publication terms.
Aya's notebook visibly separates what is on the record from what is not.
A sealed negative makes the option not to publish materially visible.
Aya's first route event records that consent is part of accurate reporting.
Aya marks the risk line before treating a wind frame as a composition exercise.
A visible exit keeps photo-graze from turning the player into evidence.
Aya records a photo frame whose safe exit is part of the reporting practice.
Aya's darkroom holds a private accountability record rather than a cache of romantic or exploitative images.
The unprinted folder records interventions without turning people into a headline.
A mended bridge rope documents an action that mattered more than a photograph.
Aya keeps decisions answerable without exposing the people she helped.
Aya posts a correction at the same public threshold where the premature story travelled.
The premature notice stays marked rather than being quietly erased.
The correction board places accountability where affected readers can find it.
Aya corrects a public error without reducing repair to deletion.
Aya can put her camera down above the Bunbunmaru office without making exhaustion a route reward.
The closed camera case makes Aya's choice to pause the cycle materially visible.
An unsent dispatch proves a story can wait without becoming a failure.
Aya's rest remains chosen and bounded rather than owed to the player.
